title: Swiss Screw Machining: High-Volume Small-Diameter Parts
preview: Swiss screw machines produce small-diameter precision parts at high volume with unmatched repeatability. Learn when to specify Swiss and what tolerances to expect.

title: CNC Turning Basics: What Every Engineer Should Know
preview: CNC turning is one of the most efficient ways to produce cylindrical parts. This guide breaks down tolerances, materials, and when turning beats milling.

title: Understanding Tolerances in CNC Machining
preview: Specifying tolerances too tight drives up cost. Too loose and parts don't fit. Here's how to find the right balance for your application.

title: Material Selection for CNC Parts: Aluminum vs Steel vs Titanium
preview: The material you choose affects machinability, lead time, and cost dramatically. A practical breakdown for engineers sourcing precision parts.

title: CNC Milling: 3-Axis, 4-Axis, and 5-Axis Explained
preview: More axes means more capability — but also more cost. A practical breakdown of when to use 3-axis vs 4-axis vs full 5-axis simultaneous machining.

title: Surface Finish in CNC Machining: Ra, Rz, and Callout Best Practices
preview: Ra 125, Ra 32, ground finish — surface finish callouts affect cost and lead time significantly. Here's how to specify only what your design actually needs.

title: Anodizing Aluminum: Type I, II, and III Compared
preview: Hard anodize, standard anodize, clear or color — choosing the right anodize type for your aluminum parts affects corrosion resistance, wear, and dimensional tolerance.

title: ITAR Compliance in CNC Machining: What Procurement Needs to Know
preview: Defense and aerospace parts often require ITAR-registered manufacturers. Here's what ITAR registration means, what it doesn't cover, and how to source compliantly.

title: Thread Standards for Machined Parts: UNC, UNF, Metric, and NPT
preview: Mixing thread standards causes costly rework. A concise reference on the most common thread callouts, tolerances, and how to specify them correctly on your drawings.

title: First Article Inspection: What to Require and What to Skip
preview: FAI reports add cost and lead time. For prototype quantities they're often overkill. Here's when to require full AS9102 FAI, and when a dimensional report is enough.

title: Heat Treatment for Machined Parts: Hardening, Tempering, and Annealing
preview: Heat treatment changes mechanical properties but also affects dimensions. When to specify heat treat before vs after machining, and what distortion to expect.

title: Stainless Steel Grades: 303 vs 304 vs 316 vs 17-4 PH
preview: Not all stainless is equal. A machinist's comparison of the four most common grades — machinability, corrosion resistance, cost, and when each is the right call.

title: Prototype to Production: How Part Design Changes When You Scale
preview: A part designed for 5 pieces often needs redesign at 5,000. Features that are fine to EDM at prototype quantities become bottlenecks at volume. What to watch for.

title: CMM Inspection Reports: What to Ask For and How to Read Them
preview: Coordinate measuring machine reports are the gold standard for dimensional verification. What a complete CMM report contains, what to flag, and when to require it.

title: Engineering Drawing Best Practices for CNC Sourcing
preview: A poor drawing slows quoting, causes misquotes, and leads to rework. The ten most common drawing mistakes and how to fix them before sending your RFQ.

title: What Actually Drives Lead Time on CNC Parts
preview: Shops quote 3–4 weeks but sometimes deliver in 5 days. Lead time is driven by material availability, machine load, and finishing ops — not just complexity.

title: AS9100 vs ISO 9001: Which Certification Do You Actually Need?
preview: Both certifications signal quality systems, but AS9100 adds aerospace-specific requirements. When to require AS9100 vs ISO 9001 — and when neither is necessary.

title: The Complete RFQ Checklist for CNC Machined Parts
preview: Missing information in an RFQ delays quotes and causes back-and-forth. A complete checklist of what to include when requesting quotes for precision machined parts.
